industries.name: Steel Manufacturing
industries.share: ~90% of manganese use — there is no substitute for manganese in steelmaking
uses: Deoxidation and desulfurization: manganese reacts with dissolved oxygen (forming MnO to slag) and sulfur (forming MnS, preventing red-shortness) in every grade of steel — minimum ~0.25% Mn in virtually all commercial steel
Solid solution strengthening: manganese in steel increases hardenability and tensile strength without significantly reducing ductility or weldability

High-manganese austenitic steel (Hadfield steel, ~13% Mn): extraordinary work-hardening capacity makes it ideal for crushing jaws, grinding mill liners, railroad switches, and excavator bucket teeth — surface hardens dramatically under impact

TWIP steel (twinning-induced plasticity, 15–25% Mn): ultra-high-strength automotive steel combining 600+ MPa strength with 50%+ elongation for crash-energy-absorbing body structures — reduces vehicle weight without sacrificing safety
Silicomanganese (SiMn) and ferromanganese (FeMn) are the commercial forms — produced by carbothermic reduction of manganese ore in submerged arc furnaces and added to steel ladles

companies.name: South32 (GEMCO)
companies.use: Operates Groote Eylandt Mining Company (GEMCO) in the Northern Territory — the world's largest single manganese ore operation. Ships high-grade Mn ore to steelmakers and ferroalloy smelters across Asia, Europe, and the Americas.

companies.name: Eramet
companies.use: Major manganese miner and ferroalloy producer. Operates the Moanda mine in Gabon (world's highest-grade large Mn ore deposit) and silicomanganese smelters in Norway, France, and China.

companies.name: Assmang (African Rainbow Minerals)
companies.use: Operates Black Rock manganese mines in the Kalahari Manganese Field — the world's largest known manganese ore body, holding ~80% of global reserves. Produces ore and ferromanganese for export.

companies.name: Compagnie Minière de l'Ogooué (COMILOG)
companies.use: Eramet subsidiary operating the Moanda mine, producing battery-grade and metallurgical manganese ore. Gabon is the world's second-largest manganese exporter, supplying global steel and battery industries.

companies.name: Vale
companies.use: Major manganese ore producer at Azul mine (Pará, Brazil) and ferroalloy smelter operator. Supplies Brazilian and global steelmakers with metallurgical manganese ore and ferromanganese.

industries.name: Batteries & Energy Storage
industries.share: ~7% of manganese use — rapidly growing with EV adoption
uses: Electrolytic manganese dioxide (EMD) as cathode material in alkaline batteries (AA, AAA, C, D cells) — every alkaline battery contains 3–5g MnO₂; global alkaline battery market consumes ~400,000 t EMD/yr

Lithium manganese oxide (LMO, LiMn₂O₄ spinel): cathode in lithium-ion batteries. Safe, thermally stable, low-cost alternative to cobalt-containing cathodes. Used in power tools, e-bikes, and some EV battery packs (Nissan Leaf early generations)

NMC (nickel manganese cobalt oxide) cathodes for EV batteries: NMC 811 (80% Ni, 10% Mn, 10% Co), NMC 622, NMC 532 — manganese improves structural stability and reduces cost relative to pure nickel or cobalt cathodes

LMFP (lithium manganese iron phosphate) — emerging high-voltage cathode combining LFP safety with higher energy density by substituting ~30% manganese for iron; BYD and CATL advancing this chemistry for EVs
High-purity manganese sulfate (HPMSM, battery-grade, >99.9% MnSO₄) is the refined precursor for NMC and LMO cathode manufacturing — distinct from metallurgical-grade Mn ore

companies.name: CATL (Contemporary Amperex Technology)
companies.use: World's largest EV battery manufacturer. Major consumer of manganese in NMC and LMFP cathode chemistries. Developing sodium-ion batteries using manganese-based cathodes as a low-cost, lithium-free alternative.

companies.name: Umicore
companies.share: ~10% of global NMC cathode material market
companies.use: Major NMC cathode material producer for EV batteries. Purchases high-purity manganese sulfate as precursor for NMC 622, NMC 811, and next-generation cathode materials supplied to Samsung SDI, LG Energy Solution, and automotive OEMs.

companies.name: Duracell (Berkshire Hathaway)
companies.use: World's largest alkaline battery brand. Each battery contains ~3–5g of electrolytic manganese dioxide (EMD) as the cathode material. Consumes tens of thousands of tonnes of EMD annually from global suppliers.

industries.name: Chemicals & Specialty Products
uses: Potassium permanganate (KMnO₄) as a powerful oxidizing agent for water treatment (iron, manganese, and H₂S removal), wastewater treatment, chemical synthesis, and medical antiseptic (Condy's crystals)
Manganese dioxide (MnO₂) as oxidant in organic chemistry, as a depolarizer in zinc-carbon dry cells, and as a pigment (manganese brown) in ceramics and glass

Methylcyclopentadienyl manganese tricarbonyl (MMT) as antiknock fuel additive for gasoline — used as an octane booster in countries where tetraethyllead is banned; controversial due to potential neurotoxicity
Manganese sulfate as micronutrient fertilizer — manganese deficiency common in high-pH soils; also used as animal feed supplement for livestock bone development and enzyme function

companies.name: Carus Chemical
companies.use: Major North American producer of potassium permanganate for municipal water treatment, industrial wastewater remediation, and chemical synthesis applications.

companies.name: Brenntag
companies.use: World's largest chemical distributor. Distributes manganese sulfate, manganese dioxide, and potassium permanganate to agricultural, water treatment, and industrial chemical markets across 78 countries.
